name: integer_bounds
expected_stdout:
MaxInt:2147483647
MinInt:-2147483648
ZeroCard:0
NegOne:-1
MaxAgain:2147483647
Div:3
Mod:1
NegDiv:-4
NegMod:1

name: mtu_linkage_trap
_comment: Multi-TU linkage trap: two modules with same-named exports (value, GetValue). Passes in both single-TU and multi-TU when names are properly module-prefixed. Would fail at link time in multi-TU if codegen emitted bare names.

name: type_cast_test
expected_stdout:
type cast OK
all casts OK
_comment: Tests ADDRESS/WORD/BYTE casts emit (void*) not function calls, and user-defined pointer type casts.

name: high_fixed_array_test
expected_stdout:
HIGH local array OK
HIGH named type OK
HIGH record char field OK
HIGH record int field OK
all HIGH OK
_comment: Tests HIGH() on fixed-size arrays: local vars, named types, and record fields.

name: c_keyword_params_test
expected_stdout:
keyword params OK
hello from default
all keyword params OK
_comment: Tests C keyword names (short, long, register, default) used as M2 parameter names get mangled.

name: proc_var_call_test
expected_stdout:
proc var call OK
record field proc call OK
all proc var calls OK
_comment: Tests calls through proc-typed variables with VAR params: simple vars and record field proc vars.

name: proc_type_open_array_test
expected_stdout:
proc type open array OK
high propagated correctly
all proc type open array OK
_comment: Tests procedure type typedefs with open array params include _high in the C typedef.

name: open_array_cross_module
expected_stdout:
fixed array high OK
fixed array fill OK
open array forward OK
all open array cross module OK
_comment: Regression: FROM-imported proc with open array param must resolve correct module's param info when another module exports a same-named proc without open arrays.

name: def_only_module
expected_stdout:
color=0
color=2
max=64
coord=10,20
init OK
setcolor OK
getcoord OK
all def-only module OK
_comment: Regression: definition-only modules (.def without .mod) must have their types emitted in the C output. Without this, modules referencing def-only types in procedure signatures get 'type not declared' errors attributed to unrelated library modules.

name: array_field_name_collision
expected_stdout:
tag=42 val=420
tag=99 val=990
tag=-7 val=-70
tag=0 val=0
direct=100,101
all array field collision OK
_comment: Regression: when two records in different modules have same-named fields with different types (one array, one scalar), arr[i].field assignment must resolve the correct record type through array indexing instead of falling back to name-only matching that would incorrectly generate memcpy for scalar fields.

name: args_getarg_high_test
_comment: Tests Args.GetArg emits _high param for open-array buffer argument.

name: try_except_basic
_comment: TRY/EXCEPT: named handler, catch-all, no-exception path, body runs once.

name: try_except_nested
expected_stdout:
outer-body
inner-body
inner-caught
outer-continues
outer-caught
try-body
finally-normal
finally-exc
outer-after-finally
done
_comment: Nested TRY/EXCEPT: inner catches own, unhandled propagates to outer, FINALLY always runs.

name: pointer_array_field_collision
_comment: Regression: array field and pointer field with same name in different records must not collide.

name: val_longint_cast
_comment: Regression: LONG() and VAL(LONGINT,x) must emit proper C cast, not bare LONG().

name: typecase_basic
expected_stdout:
int
real
char
other
int=42
real
else
_comment: TYPECASE with REF types: basic dispatch, ELSE branch, variable binding via M2_ISA.

name: typecase_object
_comment: TYPECASE with OBJECT hierarchy: subtype matching via M2_ISA parent chain walk.

name: const_char_literal
_comment: Regression: CONST x = '/'; must generate char, not const char *.

name: ptr_anon_array_index
_comment: Regression: bp^[i] for POINTER TO ARRAY must generate bp[i], not (*bp)[i].

name: scalar_array_field_collision
expected_stdout:
msg=OK
code=200
status=404
status2=200
status3=301
batch0=500
batch1=401
batch2=102
batch3=103
ok
_comment: Regression: scalar field and array field with same name in different imported records must not collide (memcpy of scalar = SIGSEGV). Covers simple var.field, array[idx].field, and loop assignment.

name: open_array_record_scalar
_comment: Regression: assigning CARDINAL field via VAR open array element must emit direct assignment, not memcpy. Covers open_array[idx].field := local.field, literal, variable, and nested record field paths.

name: struct_array_field_collision
_comment: Regression: RECORD-typed field with same name as ARRAY OF CHAR field in another record must emit struct assignment, not memcpy. Covers struct-to-struct copy and local record variable assignment.

name: enum_array_field_collision
expected_stdout:
log=OK
code=200
s1=0
d1=100
s2=0
s3=2
s4=1
dur=999
s5=3
ok
_comment: Regression: enumeration-typed field with same name as ARRAY OF CHAR field in another record must emit direct assignment, not memcpy. Covers enum literal, enum variable, field-to-field, and array-indexed patterns.

name: c_stdlib_name_clash
expected_stdout:
log=10
time=20
stat=30
errno=40
read=50
write=60
signal=14
_comment: Variables and procedures named after C stdlib functions (log, time, stat, errno, read, write, signal) must be mangled to avoid C identifier clashes.

name: var_addr_array_leak
_comment: Regression: local ARRAY variable named 'key' in one embedded procedure must not leak into array_vars and cause a VAR ADDRESS parameter named 'key' in another procedure to emit memcpy instead of pointer assignment.

name: variant_else
_comment: PIM4 variant record ELSE clause for default variant fields.

name: case_leading_pipe
_comment: PIM4 CASE statement with optional leading pipe before first branch.

name: variant_leading_pipe
_comment: PIM4 variant record with optional leading pipe before first variant.

name: nested_variant
_comment: PIM4 nested CASE variant records with field access through nested variants.

name: subrange_base_type
_comment: PIM4 subrange type with base type qualifier: INTEGER [-10..10], CARDINAL [0..99].

name: nul_char_assign
_comment: PIM4: empty string '' or "" assigned to CHAR yields NUL (CHR(0)); single-char string to CHAR.

name: char_subrange_assign
_comment: PIM4: single-char string literals assignable to CHAR subrange types like ['A'..'Z'].

name: char_high_bit_ord
expected_stdout:
ORD values:
 128
 129
 200
 254
 255
   0
  65
 127
Verify 128: PASS
Verify 255: PASS
Index test: PASS
Done
_comment: Ensures ORD(char) zero-extends high-bit chars (0x80-0xFF), not sign-extends. C backend bug fix.

name: proc_type_equiv
_comment: PIM4: procedure type alias equivalence — aliasedType = functionType should be assignment-compatible.

name: address_div_mod
_comment: PIM4: DIV and MOD operators accept ADDRESS operands from SYSTEM.

name: proc_builtin_type
_comment: PIM4: PROC is a built-in type alias for parameterless PROCEDURE.

name: type_alias_forward
_comment: PIM4: type alias to ARRAY type resolved correctly (not treated as opaque).

name: ptr_to_record
_comment: POINTER TO anonymous RECORD with self-referential field and field access.

name: with_ptr_record
_comment: WITH on dereferenced POINTER TO anonymous RECORD.

name: set_char_ops
_comment: INCL/EXCL/IN with enum elements on SET, and char literal array index.

name: set_inline_enum
_comment: SET OF enumeration type: enum constants emitted and set ops work.

name: multi_ptr_field
_comment: Multiple pointer-typed field names in one record declaration.

name: var_forward_ref
_comment: Procedure referencing module-level VAR declared after the procedure.

name: const_forward_ref
_comment: Constants referencing later-declared constants (forward references).

name: nested_module_proc
_comment: Local module inside program with exported procedure.

name: nested_proc_collision
_comment: Same-named nested procedures in different parent procedures.

expected_stdout:
3: small

200: out of range
H: uppercase
x: lowercase
5: digit
!: other

name: multidim_array_index
_comment: Multi-dimensional array ARRAY [1..N],[1..N] with constant bounds and m[i][j] access.

name: matrix_multidim
_comment: Matrix operations on multi-dimensional REAL arrays with constant bounds.

name: longcard_div_mod
expected_stdout:
bytes: 0 0 1 149 141 3 238 128
roundtrip: PASS
proc params: PASS
alias params: PASS
large div mod: PASS
val cardinal: PASS
all longcard div mod OK

c_scan_patterns._note: LONGCARD DIV/MOD must emit plain C / and %, never m2_div() or m2_mod() which truncate to int32_t

_comment: Regression: m2_div(int32_t,int32_t) and m2_mod(int32_t,int32_t) were called with 64-bit LONGCARD operands, silently truncating values. LONGCARD DIV/MOD must emit plain C unsigned / and %.

name: longint_div_mod
expected_stdout:
large positive: PASS
large non-exact: PASS
(-7) DIV 2 = -4  MOD = 1
floor div small: PASS
floor div 64: PASS
neg mod pos: PASS
proc floor 64: PASS
proc large pos: PASS
all longint div mod OK

c_scan_patterns._note: LONGINT DIV/MOD must emit m2_div64/m2_mod64 (64-bit floored), never m2_div/m2_mod (32-bit truncation)

_comment: Regression: m2_div(int32_t,int32_t) and m2_mod(int32_t,int32_t) were called with 64-bit LONGINT operands, silently truncating values. LONGINT DIV/MOD must emit m2_div64/m2_mod64 for correct PIM4 floored semantics.

name: for_char_literal
_comment: Regression: single-char string literals in FOR loop bounds were emitted as C string literals (char[2]) instead of char values, causing incompatible pointer-to-integer conversion.

name: func_call_index
_comment: Regression: function call as array index on LHS was emitted as 0 in C backend, writing to wrong index.

name: enum_indexed_array
_comment: Regression: ARRAY EnumType OF T emitted zero-size C arrays ([/* size */]) causing stack corruption. Now emits [m2_max_EnumName + 1].

name: func_deref
expected_stdout:
basic: 10
assign: 20
double: 30
expr: 42
elem0: 100
elem2: 300
_comment: Regression: Func(x)^ was a parse error. Tests basic deref, assign through deref, double deref (^^), deref in arithmetic, and array element deref via function.

name: address_index
expected_stdout:
r0=H
r1=e
r2=l
r3=l
r4=o
mod=World
shift=rld
_comment: Regression: ADDRESS^[i] byte-level indexing emitted void* indexing in C backend, causing incomplete type error. m2plus feature: treat ADDRESS as POINTER TO ARRAY OF CHAR for indexing.

name: param_entry_clash
_comment: Regression: LLVM backend emitted entry: as the block label which clashes with a parameter named entry, causing unable to create block named entry.

name: stdlib_proc_val
_comment: Regression: LLVM backend emitted load from @Args_GetArg instead of using @m2_Args_GetArg as function pointer when stdlib procedure passed as proc value argument.

name: record_cross_module
_comment: Regression: LLVM backend emitted void for unresolved imported enum types in array elements inside records, and wrong deref type for POINTER TO ADDRESS across modules.

name: record_param_cross
_comment: Regression: LLVM backend mismatched types when passing record by value to cross-module procedure. Tests record with proc type field, record return, and callback dispatch.

name: cross_module_name_clash
expected_stdout:
code=200
hcount=2
ok=0
err=1
conn=0
epst=0
_comment: Regression: two modules with same impl-only type names (ConnRec/ConnPtr), nested deref chains (c^.resp^.headerCount), re-exported enums (NetB re-exports NetA.Status), INC on deeply nested fields. Tests LLVM backend type disambiguation and enum variant resolution.

name: proc_field_call
_comment: Indirect call through procedure-type field in record via pointer dereference.

name: main_module_types
expected_stdout:
port=8080
mode=1
node=42
item0=3000
buf=hello
cb=35
si=200
set=ok
cnt=999
_comment: All major type declaration kinds in main program module — exercises gen_type_decl_from_id.

name: proc_local_decls
expected_stdout:
buf=hello
color=1
const=18
cbuf=hi
nested=37
vec=600
_comment: PIM4 proc-local TYPE, CONST, VAR, nested PROCEDURE with closures.
